text: The surgeon forms the Roux limb by dividing the jejunum 30 centimeters from the ligamentum teres. A stapler is fired over the mesentery to mobilize the limb towards the stomach. A side-to-side anastomosis is initiated 90 centimeters from the cut end of the Roux limb using an endolinear stapler, creating an opening with the retrocolic Roux limb for the gastrojejunostomy.

The surgeon initiates the creation of the gastric pouch by using an endogenous stapler to form a horizontal row, followed by a vertical row with a blue cartridge. A linear stapler is used to perform an end-to-side anastomosis, fixing the Roux limb to the stomach. The opening of the gastrointestinal anastomosis is then closed with sutures.
Thank you very much for watching just a brief demonstration. of Roux-en-Y gastric bypass.
